concerns.text: Task T019 ('project external frequency distributions...') contradicts the Plan summary which states 'projecting *model token embeddings* (not frequency distributions)'. The Spec (FR-005, US-2) explicitly requires projecting 'external frequency distributions' to compute the 'mean embedding'. The task description is correct, but the Plan summary introduces a semantic weakening of the methodology. If the implementation follows the Plan summary text, it violates FR-005.

Task T025 implements a 'Within-Language Baseline' (Llama-3 vs. Mistral) as the null distribution. The Spec (FR-004, US-3) mandates a 'model-seed null distribution' (same-language, different-seed). The Plan admits seed-variant models are unavailable and substitutes the baseline. This is a semantic weakening of the statistical rigor required by FR-004. The task must explicitly document this deviation as a 'staged simplification' or the spec must be amended to accept the baseline as a valid proxy, otherwise the acceptance criteria for US-3 (statistical significance) are untestable as defined.

Task T028 allows for 'mock validation for schema compliance' if WALS data is unavailable. Spec SC-004 requires measuring validity against the 'correlation coefficient between the subspace orientation and external, non-LLM-derived typological features'. A mock validation produces no correlation coefficient, failing the success criterion. This is a silent relaxation of the validation requirement.

The Spec (SC-001) requires measuring anisotropy deviation against a 'hypothesized null (0.95)' and including a 'confidence interval'. The tasks for US-1 (T011-T015) only mention outputting 'cosine similarity scores'. There is no task to calculate the deviation from 0.95 or compute the confidence interval. This drops the acceptance criteria for SC-001.
